name: HOGWARTS RPG | The Dark Romance Hogwarts | The Shadows of Hogwarts | A Dark RPG
description:
🕯️ Welcome to Hogwarts: A Darkening Legacy

⚰️ An RPG of Gothic Romance & Visceral Realism

~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~⋅~

A Gothic Romance in the Shadow of War

Forget the children's tales.

This is not the Hogwarts of your memory—not the place of whimsy and wonder where the greatest dangers were detention and lost House points. That school exists somewhere in the amber of nostalgia, preserved and false. What stands before you now is something older, something that has always lurked beneath the cheerful veneer: a fortress of ancient stone where the shadows move with purpose, where alliances are sealed in silence and broken in blood, where every choice carries weight that cannot be undone.

You are not a child stepping wide-eyed onto Platform Nine and Three-Quarters. You are a young adult—eighteen, nineteen, twenty—entering a castle that is both sanctuary and gilded cage, and you enter it at the dawn of open war.

The year is 1996. The Sixth Year begins.

Sirius Black is dead. The Ministry can no longer maintain its comfortable denial; the Second Wizarding War has been declared, acknowledged, made official and inescapable. The atmosphere is not one of suppressed paranoia but of overt grief and mounting terror. There are empty seats in the Great Hall. There are names that are no longer spoken aloud. The air is thick with mourning, and beneath the mourning, the slow cold creep of what is coming.

This is the world you inherit. This is the story you will write.

On the Architecture of This World

Four engines breathe beneath the surface of this narrative, invisible and tireless, shaping everything you encounter.

The Timeline Engine tracks every day of the Sixth Year with the precision of a chronicler who has lived it. September through June, breakfast through curfew, Hogsmeade weekends and holiday breaks—the calendar turns whether you watch it or not. Events fire on schedule. Canon beats arrive when their hour comes. And if you leap forward through time, the Engine ensures you inherit what occurred in your absence: the cursed necklace, the poisoned mead, the whispered rumours that became screaming headlines. The world does not pause when you look away.

The World Engine maintains the rules of this darkened place—the weight of magic (for spellwork here has cost, and overuse leaves its mark), the rhythm of patrols and the gradations of consequence, the atmospheric palette of each month as war pressure mounts. It tracks security levels and paranoia, enforcement styles and witness pressure. It offers plot hooks without forcing them, seeds subplots that may bloom or wither depending on your attention. It ensures that the castle feels like a fortress pretending to be a school.

The Relationship Engine keeps the ledger of hearts. Every warmth extended, every cruelty absorbed, every bid for connection answered or ignored—it remembers. Trust accrues slowly and bleeds quickly. Resentment compounds in silence. A single apology may unwind weeks of tension, or it may land on ground too salted to receive it. The Engine tracks each bond separately, each pairing as its own living history, and it ensures that characters respond to you based on what you have built with them—or what you have broken.

The Census Engine populates the world. When you enter the Great Hall, it asks: Who would be here? When you walk the dungeons after curfew, it considers: Who patrols these corridors? It knows every professor and prefect, every ghost and groundskeeper, every student from every House and year. It selects presences based on location, time, faction, and narrative weight. It ensures that Hogwarts feels full—not a stage dressed with only the principals, but a living institution
creator: Elly Gumileva
